title: Baseline support for covergroups, coverpoints, and bins
body:
This PR adds support for SystemVerilog covergroups, coverpoints, bins, and cross-points. It extends Verilator's coverage database to represent functional (covergroup) coverage in addition to the already-supported coverage types. This PR also targets #7099 

The code and tests in this PR were prepared using Copilot under my direction and with my review.

Here is an assessment of feature coverage vs the full set of SystemVerilog functional coverage features from the 1800-2023:

# SystemVerilog Functional Coverage — Annotated Feature Checklist

This document follows the structure of `sv_functional_coverage_features.md` and annotates each
feature and feature combination with its current implementation status in Verilator.

**Status legend**
- ✅ **SUPPORTED** — implemented and covered by a passing test
- ⚠️ **PARTIAL** — partially implemented; gap noted inline
- ❌ **UNSUPPORTED** — explicitly rejected (`E_UNSUPPORTED` / `COVERIGN` warning) or no implementation found
- 🔲 **PARSED ONLY** — syntax accepted and stored in AST; no effect on code generation or simulation

---

## 1. `covergroup` Declaration

### 1.1 Sampling / Triggering Event

- ✅ No sampling event (implicit `sample()` method, no arguments)
- ✅ Clocking event (`@(posedge clk)`, `@(negedge clk)`, `@(signal)`) — module-level signal
  - ❌ Clocking event references a covergroup formal argument — depends on class-member clocking (unsupported)
  - 🔲 Clocking event + `strobe` type option — `type_option.strobe` parsed, not applied
- ✅ `with function sample(...)` — overridden sample method with arguments
  - ✅ Sample method with `input` arguments
  - ❌ Sample method with `ref` arguments — no implementation
  - ✅ Sample method invoked procedurally
  - ❌ Sample method invoked from within a property / assertion
  - ❌ Sample method invoked from within an automatic task/function
- ❌ Block event expression (`@@ (begin hierarchical_id)`) — `COVERIGN` "coverage '@@' events"
- ❌ Block event expression (`@@ (end hierarchical_id)`) — same
- ❌ Block event expression with `or` (multiple begin/end events) — same

> **Section summary:** Procedural-`sample()` and module-level clocking events are fully supported.
> Block events and class-member clocking events are unsupported. `ref` sample arguments and
> assertion-triggered sampling are unimplemented. `strobe` type option is parsed only.

### 1.2 Formal Arguments

- ✅ No formal arguments
- ✅ `input` formal argument (value captured at `new` time)
- ❌ `ref` formal argument (tracks changes after construction)
- ✅ Default argument values
- ✅ Formal argument used in coverpoint expression
- ✅ Formal argument used in bin range
- 🔲 Formal argument used in coverage option (e.g., `option.weight = arg`) — option assignment parsed, not applied
- ⚠️ Formal argument used in `iff` guard expression — structural support present; not explicitly tested

> **Section summary:** `input` args (including defaults) and use in coverpoint/bin expressions are
> fully supported. `ref` args are unimplemented. Use in coverage options is parsed only.

### 1.3 Instantiation

- ✅ Single instance (`cg cg_inst = new;`)
- ✅ Multiple instances of same covergroup type
- ✅ Instance with actual arguments to `new`
- 🔲 Instance name set via `option.name` — parsed; not reflected in database
- ⚠️ Instance name set via `set_inst_name()` — stub method; empty body; no effect

> **Section summary:** All instantiation patterns are supported. Naming via `option.name` or
> `set_inst_name()` has no functional effect.

### 1.4 Placement / Scope

- ✅ Defined in a module
- ❌ Defined in a program — `program` blocks broadly unsupported in Verilator
- ❌ Defined in an interface — no tests found
- ❌ Defined in a checker — no tests found
- ❌ Defined in a package — no tests found
- ✅ Defined in a class (embedded covergroup — see §1.5)

> **Section summary:** Module-level and class-embedded covergroups are supported.
> Program, interface, checker, and package scopes are unsupported.

### 1.5 Embedded Covergroup (in a class)

- ✅ Embedded covergroup — anonymous type, named variable
- ✅ Embedded covergroup referencing `local` class members
- ✅ Embedded covergroup referencing `protected` class members
- ✅ Embedded covergroup referencing public class members
- ❌ Embedded covergroup with clocking event referencing a class member — `COVERIGN`
- ❌ Explicit instantiation in `new()` after dependent object is created — ordering dependency untested
- 🔲 Formal argument used to set a coverage option from `new(p1)` — option not applied

> **Section summary:** Class-embedded covergroups with `with function sample(args)` or
> module-level clocking are supported. Class-member clocking events are rejected.

### 1.6 Embedded Covergroup Inheritance (`extends`)

- ❌ Derived covergroup adds a new coverpoint
- ❌ Derived covergroup overrides a base coverpoint (same name)
- ❌ Derived covergroup overrides a base coverage option
- ❌ Derived covergroup inherits (keeps) unoverridden base options
- ❌ Cross in base covergroup still active when base coverpoint overridden
- ❌ Multi-level inheritance (derived of derived)
- ❌ `get_coverage()` treats base and derived types separately

> **Section summary:** Covergroup inheritance (`extends`) is entirely unsupported.
> `COVERIGN` "covergroup inheritance (extends)" — covergroup silently dropped.

### 1.7 Covergroup-Level Coverage Options

- 🔲 `option.name`
- 🔲 `option.weight` — parsed; weighted-average computation not implemented
- 🔲 `option.goal`
- 🔲 `option.comment`
- ✅ `option.at_least`
- ✅ `option.auto_bin_max` (default for coverpoints)
- 🔲 `option.cross_num_print_missing` (default for crosses)
- 🔲 `option.cross_retain_auto_bins` (default for crosses)
- 🔲 `option.detect_overlap` (default for coverpoints)
- 🔲 `option.per_instance`
- 🔲 `option.get_inst_coverage`
- 🔲 `type_option.weight`
- 🔲 `type_option.goal`
- 🔲 `type_option.comment`
- 🔲 `type_option.strobe`
- 🔲 `type_option.merge_instances`
- 🔲 `type_option.distribute_first`
- 🔲 `type_option.real_interval` (default for coverpoints)
- ⚠️ Option set in covergroup definition — `at_least` and `auto_bin_max` take effect; all others parsed only
- ❌ Option set procedurally after instantiation (`inst.option.x = ...`) — no mechanism post-elaboration
- ❌ Type option set via `::` scope (`CgType::type_option.x = ...`) — no mechanism

> **Section summary:** Only `option.at_least` and `option.auto_bin_max` have functional effect.
> All other options (including all `type_option.*`) are parsed and stored but have no effect on
> code generation, computation, or the coverage database.

---

## 2. `coverpoint`

### 2.1 Expression Type

- ✅ Single variable (integral)
- ✅ Arbitrary integral expression
- ❌ Real (floating-point) expression — no real-type comparison or bin logic
- ❌ Clocking block signal (samples from a specific scheduling region) — no scheduling-region handling

> **Section summary:** All integral expressions are supported. Real expressions and clocking-block
> signal sampling are unimplemented.

### 2.2 Naming

- ✅ Labeled coverpoint (`label: coverpoint expr`)
- ✅ Unlabeled coverpoint of a single variable (variable name becomes coverpoint name)
- ✅ Unlabeled coverpoint of an expression (implementation-generated name)

> **Section summary:** Fully supported.

### 2.3 Explicit Data Type

- ✅ No explicit data type (inferred from expression)
- ⚠️ Explicit integral data type (`bit [N:0] label: coverpoint expr`) — syntax accepted; type annotation not yet used to influence bin value resolution (signed/unsigned boundaries)

> **Section summary:** Inferred types are fully supported. Explicit type annotation is parsed
> but does not yet affect bin range or value-resolution behavior.

### 2.4 `iff` Guard on Coverpoint

- ✅ `coverpoint expr iff (condition)` — coverage ignored when condition is false
- ✅ `iff` references a signal
- ⚠️ `iff` references a covergroup formal argument — structural support; not explicitly tested
- ✅ `iff` combined with explicit bins
- ✅ `iff` combined with automatic bins

> **Section summary:** Fully supported for all practical cases. Formal-argument reference in
> `iff` is untested but structurally supported.

### 2.5 Automatic Bin Creation (no explicit bins)

- ✅ Automatic bins for an enum type (one bin per enum value)
- ✅ Automatic bins for an integral type (up to `auto_bin_max` bins)
- ✅ `auto_bin_max` option at coverpoint level
- ✅ `auto_bin_max` option at covergroup level (default for all coverpoints)
- ✅ Automatic bins with `ignore_bins` present (some bins may empty)
- ✅ Automatic bins with `illegal_bins` present
- ✅ 2-state value filtering (x/z excluded from automatic bins)

> **Section summary:** Fully supported.

### 2.6 State Bins (`bins` / value bins)

#### 2.6.1 Bin Cardinality

- ✅ Single aggregate bin (no `[]`): `bins name = { ... }`
- ✅ Open array of bins (`[]`): `bins name[] = { ... }` — one bin per value
- ✅ Fixed-count array of bins (`[N]`): `bins name[N] = { ... }` — N bins, values distributed
- ✅ Fixed count smaller than number of values (uniform distribution, last bin gets remainder)
- ✅ Fixed count larger than number of values (some bins are empty)

> **Section summary:** Fully supported.

#### 2.6.2 Value Specification

- ✅ Single value: `{ val }`
- ✅ Value list: `{ v1, v2, v3 }`
- ✅ Closed range: `{ [lo:hi] }`
- ⚠️ Range with `$` (min): `{ [$:hi] }` — handled via `InsideRange`; simple cases work; corner cases untested
- ⚠️ Range with `$` (max): `{ [lo:$] }` — same caveat
- ✅ Overlapping ranges across bins
- ✅ Enum literal values in range list
- ✅ Expression values in range list (covergroup expressions)

> **Section summary:** Mostly supported. `$`-bounded ranges work for common cases but have
> untested edge cases.

#### 2.6.3 Real-Value Bins

- ❌ Single real value bin
- ❌ Real range bin (`[lo:hi]`) — divided into intervals by `real_interval`
- ❌ Real range smaller than interval size (single bin)
- ❌ Real range equal to interval size (single bin)
- ❌ Real range larger than interval size (multiple bins)
- ❌ Multiple real ranges — identical intervals merged
- ❌ `$` in real range (assigned to a single bin, no interval division)
- ❌ Absolute tolerance: `{[val +/- tol]}`
- ❌ Relative tolerance: `{[val +%- tol]}`
- ❌ `type_option.real_interval` set at coverpoint level
- ❌ `default` bin for real coverpoint (no `[]` allowed)
- ❌ No `default[]` for real coverpoint (error)

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported — real expression coverpoints have no implementation.

#### 2.6.4 `with` Filter Expression on State Bins

- ❌ `bins name[] = { range } with (item % N == 0)` — `COVERIGN` "cover bin 'with' specification"; bin dropped
- ❌ `bins name[] = coverpoint_name with (expr)` — same
- ❌ `type_option.distribute_first` — `with` unsupported
- ❌ `with` not allowed for real-type bins

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported — `COVERIGN` warning; bins silently dropped.

#### 2.6.5 Set Expression (`set_covergroup_expression`)

- ❌ `bins name = set_expr` — bin values from an array-returning expression
- ❌ Set expression using fixed array
- ❌ Set expression using dynamic array or queue

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported.

#### 2.6.6 Per-Bin `iff` Guard on State Bins

- ❌ `bins name = { ... } iff (condition)` — `AstCoverBin::iffp` parsed and stored; never applied in code-gen
- ❌ `ignore_bins name = { ... } iff (condition)` — same
- ❌ `illegal_bins name = { ... } iff (condition)` — same

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported — per-bin `iff` is parsed into the AST but the
> code generator only applies the coverpoint-level `iff`, not the bin-level `iff`.

### 2.7 `default` Bin

- ✅ `bins name = default` — catches values not in any other bin
- ✅ `default` bin excluded from coverage computation
- ✅ `default` bin excluded from cross coverage
- ✅ `default` bin cannot be explicitly ignored

> **Section summary:** Fully supported.

### 2.8 Transition Bins

#### 2.8.1 Basic Transition

- ✅ Simple two-value transition: `bins name = (a => b)`
- ✅ Multi-step transition sequence: `bins name = (a => b => c => ...)`
- ✅ Range-to-range transition: `bins name = (r1 => r2)` (expands to all pairs)
- ❌ Transition not allowed for real-type coverpoints (real coverpoints entirely unsupported)

> **Section summary:** Basic and multi-step transitions with value ranges are fully supported.

#### 2.8.2 Repetition Operators

- ❌ Consecutive repetition — exact: `val [* N]` — `E_UNSUPPORTED`
- ❌ Consecutive repetition — range: `val [* M:N]` — `E_UNSUPPORTED`
- ❌ Goto repetition — exact: `val [-> N]` — `E_UNSUPPORTED`
- ❌ Goto repetition — range: `val [-> M:N]` — `E_UNSUPPORTED`
- ❌ Nonconsecutive repetition — exact: `val [= N]` — `E_UNSUPPORTED`
- ❌ Nonconsecutive repetition — range: `val [= M:N]` — `E_UNSUPPORTED`
- ❌ Repetition combined with additional transitions (e.g., `a => b [-> N] => c`) — `E_UNSUPPORTED`

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported — all three repetition operators emit
> `E_UNSUPPORTED` "Transition repetition operators not yet supported".

#### 2.8.3 Transition Bin Cardinality

- ✅ Single bin (no `[]`): all listed sequences map to one bin
- ✅ Open array of bins (`[]`): one bin per sequence
- ✅ Fixed-length-only sequences with `[]` (unbounded repetition not allowed with `[]`)

> **Section summary:** Fully supported for fixed-length sequences.

#### 2.8.4 `default sequence` Bin

- ❌ `bins name = default sequence` — `COVERIGN` "cover bin 'default' 'sequence'"

> **Section summary:** Unsupported.

#### 2.8.5 Per-Bin `iff` Guard on Transition Bins

- ❌ `bins name = (trans) iff (condition)` — per-bin `iff` parsed, not applied in transition codegen
- ❌ `ignore_bins name = (trans) iff (condition)` — same
- ❌ `illegal_bins name = (trans) iff (condition)` — same

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported — same root cause as §2.6.6.

#### 2.8.6 Wildcard Transition Bins

- ❌ `wildcard bins name = (2'b0x => 2'b1x)` — `COVERIGN` "cover bin 'wildcard' trans list"
- ❌ Wildcard transition bin open array: `wildcard bins name[] = (...)`
- ❌ Wildcard bits excluded from 2-state comparison (x/z values ignored in sampling)

> **Section summary:** Unsupported — `COVERIGN` warning for all wildcard transition bins.

### 2.9 `ignore_bins`

- ✅ `ignore_bins` for a set of values
- ✅ `ignore_bins` for a transition sequence
- ✅ `ignore_bins` interaction with state bins (values removed from other bins)
- ✅ `ignore_bins` interaction with transition bins (matching sequences removed)
- ✅ `ignore_bins` that empties an existing bin (excluded from coverage)
- ❌ `ignore_bins` with `iff` guard — per-bin `iff` not applied (see §2.6.6)
- ✅ `ignore_bins` with illegal transition sequence (unbounded not allowed — error issued)

> **Section summary:** Fully supported for values and transitions. Per-bin `iff` guard
> on `ignore_bins` is unimplemented.

### 2.10 `illegal_bins`

- ✅ `illegal_bins` for a set of values
- ✅ `illegal_bins` for a transition sequence
- ✅ `illegal_bins` causes run-time error when matched (generates `$error` + `$stop`)
- ✅ `illegal_bins` takes precedence over other bins
- ✅ `illegal_bins` interaction with state bins (values removed from other bins)
- ✅ `illegal_bins` interaction with transition bins (matching sequences removed)
- ✅ `illegal_bins` that empties an existing bin (excluded from coverage)
- ❌ `illegal_bins` with `iff` guard — per-bin `iff` not applied (see §2.6.6)
- ✅ `illegal_bins` with illegal transition sequence (unbounded not allowed — error issued)

> **Section summary:** Fully supported for values and transitions. Per-bin `iff` guard
> on `illegal_bins` is unimplemented.

### 2.11 Wildcard Value Bins

- ✅ `wildcard bins name = { 4'b11?? }` — mask-based comparison `(expr & mask) == (val & mask)`
- ✅ Wildcard value bin open array: `wildcard bins name[] = { ... }`
- ❌ Wildcard not allowed for real coverpoints — real coverpoints entirely unsupported
- ✅ 2-state filtering (x/z sampled values excluded from wildcard bin matching)
- ❌ `wildcard` + `with` filter — `COVERIGN` "cover bin 'wildcard' 'with' specification"

> **Section summary:** Wildcard value bins are fully supported. Wildcard transition bins
> and `wildcard` + `with` are unsupported.

### 2.12 Coverpoint-Level Coverage Options

- 🔲 `option.weight` — parsed; weighted-average computation not implemented
- 🔲 `option.goal`
- 🔲 `option.comment`
- ✅ `option.at_least`
- ✅ `option.auto_bin_max`
- 🔲 `option.detect_overlap`
- 🔲 `type_option.weight`
- 🔲 `type_option.goal`
- 🔲 `type_option.comment`
- 🔲 `type_option.real_interval`

> **Section summary:** Only `at_least` and `auto_bin_max` have functional effect; all others
> are parsed only.

### 2.13 Value Resolution (bin type compatibility)

- ❌ Unsigned coverpoint with signed bin value (warning issued)
- ❌ Value out of range of coverpoint type (warning issued, excluded)
- ❌ Range partially out of range (intersection kept, warning issued)
- ✅ Wildcard bins: x/z in bin treated as 0 and 1 before type check

> **Section summary:** Type-compatibility warnings and value-range intersection logic are
> unimplemented. Wildcard x/z handling is correct.

---

## 3. `cross` Coverage

### 3.1 Cross Items

- ✅ Cross of two integral variables (implicit coverpoints created)
- ✅ Cross of two explicit coverpoints
- ✅ Cross of a variable and an explicit coverpoint
- ✅ 3-way (or N-way) cross
- ❌ Cross of a real-expression coverpoint (real coverpoints unsupported)
- ✅ Cross item referencing an expression coverpoint (labeled)

> **Section summary:** All integral cross configurations are supported. Real-expression
> coverpoints in cross are unsupported.

### 3.2 Naming

- ✅ Labeled cross (`label: cross a, b`)
- ✅ Unlabeled cross

> **Section summary:** Fully supported.

### 3.3 `iff` Guard on Cross

- ❌ `cross a, b iff (condition)` — `COVERIGN` "cross iff condition"; guard silently dropped
- ❌ Per-bin `iff` guard: `bins name = select_expr iff (condition)` — cross select expressions unsupported

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported.

### 3.4 Automatic Cross Bins (no user-defined bins)

- ✅ Automatic cross bins = Cartesian product of crossed coverpoint bins
- ✅ Automatic cross bins excluded for `default`, `ignore_bins`, `illegal_bins` coverpoint bins
- ❌ Auto cross bins from coverpoints that only have auto bins — coverpoint `AUTO` type bins excluded from cross; zero cross bins generated
- 🔲 `cross_retain_auto_bins = 1` — parsed; not acted upon
- 🔲 `cross_retain_auto_bins = 0` — parsed; not acted upon
- 🔲 `cross_num_print_missing` option — parsed; not acted upon

> **Section summary:** Automatic cross bins are generated from explicit (`USER`) coverpoint bins.
> Coverpoints that use only auto-generated bins do not contribute to the cross. The
> `cross_retain_auto_bins` option is parsed only.

### 3.5 User-Defined Cross Bins (`bins_selection`)

#### 3.5.1 `binsof` Expressions

- ❌ `binsof(coverpoint)` — `COVERIGN` "coverage select expression 'binsof'"
- ❌ `binsof(variable)`
- ❌ `binsof(coverpoint.bin)`
- ❌ `binsof(coverpoint) intersect { range_list }` — `COVERIGN` "coverage select expression 'intersect'"
- ❌ `!binsof(coverpoint) intersect { range_list }`
- ❌ `binsof` on a transition bin coverpoint (uses last value of transition)

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported — all `binsof` forms produce `COVERIGN` warnings.

#### 3.5.2 Boolean Combinations of `binsof`

- ❌ `expr1 && expr2` — `COVERIGN` "coverage select expression '&&'"
- ❌ `expr1 || expr2` — `COVERIGN` "coverage select expression '||'"
- ❌ `!expr` — `COVERIGN` "coverage select expression '!'"
- ❌ Nested parenthesized combinations

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported.

#### 3.5.3 `with` on Cross Bins

- ❌ `select_expr with (condition)` — `COVERIGN` "coverage select expression with"
- ❌ `select_expr with (condition) matches N`
- ❌ `select_expr with (condition) matches $`
- ❌ `cross_identifier with (condition)`
- ❌ `with` on a `binsof` sub-expression

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported.

#### 3.5.4 Cross Set Expression

- ❌ `bins name = cross_set_expression` — queue of `CrossValType` defines bin
- ❌ `bins name = cross_set_expression matches N`
- ❌ `bins name = cross_set_expression matches $`
- ❌ Function declared inside cross body returning `CrossQueueType`
- ❌ `CrossValType` struct (one field per crossed coverpoint)
- ❌ `CrossQueueType` (queue of `CrossValType`)

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported.

#### 3.5.5 User-Defined and Auto Bins Coexisting

- ❌ Some cross products covered by user bins, rest by retained auto bins — requires `binsof` (unsupported)
- ❌ All cross products covered by user bins (no auto bins remain) — requires `binsof` (unsupported)

> **Section summary:** Unsupported — user-defined cross bin expressions require `binsof`.

### 3.6 `ignore_bins` on Cross

- ❌ `ignore_bins name = select_expression` — requires `binsof` (unsupported)
- ❌ `ignore_bins` overrides `bins` (ignored even if also in a user bin) — same dependency

> **Section summary:** Unsupported.

### 3.7 `illegal_bins` on Cross

- ❌ `illegal_bins name = select_expression` — requires `binsof` (unsupported)
- ❌ `illegal_bins` takes precedence over `ignore_bins` and other bins — same dependency

> **Section summary:** Unsupported.

### 3.8 Cross-Level Coverage Options

- 🔲 `option.weight`
- 🔲 `option.goal`
- 🔲 `option.comment`
- ⚠️ `option.at_least` — parsed; not currently applied per-cross-bin
- 🔲 `option.cross_num_print_missing`
- 🔲 `option.cross_retain_auto_bins`
- 🔲 `type_option.weight`
- 🔲 `type_option.goal`
- 🔲 `type_option.comment`

> **Section summary:** All cross-level options are parsed only. `at_least` is not applied
> to cross bins.

---

## 4. Coverage Instance Options (`option.*`)

- 🔲 `option.name`
- 🔲 `option.weight` — parsed; weighted-average computation not implemented
- 🔲 `option.goal`
- 🔲 `option.comment`
- ✅ `option.at_least` — applied in `get_inst_coverage()` bin counting
- ✅ `option.auto_bin_max` — controls bin count
- 🔲 `option.cross_num_print_missing`
- 🔲 `option.cross_retain_auto_bins`
- 🔲 `option.detect_overlap`
- 🔲 `option.per_instance`
- 🔲 `option.get_inst_coverage`
- ⚠️ Option set in covergroup definition — `at_least` and `auto_bin_max` take effect; all others no-op
- ❌ Option set procedurally on an instance (`inst.option.x = val`)
- ❌ Option set on a coverpoint of an instance (`inst.cp.option.x = val`)
- 🔲 `per_instance` and `get_inst_coverage` can only be set in covergroup definition
- 🔲 `auto_bin_max`, `detect_overlap`, `cross_retain_auto_bins` only in definition
- ⚠️ Weight = 0: item excluded from coverage computation — weight not applied in computation

> **Section summary:** Only `at_least` and `auto_bin_max` have functional effect. No
> procedural option assignment mechanism exists post-elaboration.

---

## 5. Coverage Type Options (`type_option.*`)

- 🔲 `type_option.weight`
- 🔲 `type_option.goal`
- 🔲 `type_option.comment`
- 🔲 `type_option.strobe`
- 🔲 `type_option.merge_instances`
- 🔲 `type_option.distribute_first`
- 🔲 `type_option.real_interval` — real coverpoints unsupported
- 🔲 Type option set in covergroup definition
- ❌ Type option set procedurally via `::` scope (`CgType::type_option.x = val`)
- ❌ Type option set on specific coverpoint (`CgType::cp::type_option.x = val`)
- 🔲 `strobe` and `real_interval` can only be set in covergroup definition

> **Section summary:** All type options are parsed only — none influence code generation,
> coverage computation, or the coverage database. Static `::` assignment is unimplemented.

---

## 6. Coverage Methods

### 6.1 `sample()`

- ✅ `cg_inst.sample()` — triggers sampling procedurally
- ✅ Overridden `sample(args)` (see §1.1)
- ⚠️ `start()` — stub method; empty body; no functional effect
- ⚠️ `stop()` — stub method; empty body; no functional effect
- ❌ `start()` / `stop()` on coverpoint — no coverpoint-level enable/disable

> **Section summary:** `sample()` and overridden `sample(args)` are fully supported.
> `start()`/`stop()` are stubs with no effect.

### 6.2 `get_coverage()`

- ⚠️ Called on covergroup instance (`.` operator) — method exists; always returns 0.0 (no instance registry)
- ⚠️ Called on covergroup type (`::` operator) — same; static stub; always 0.0
- ⚠️ Called on coverpoint instance — method signature exists; returns 0.0
- ⚠️ Called on cross instance — same
- ❌ With optional `(ref int covered, ref int total)` arguments — two-argument overload not implemented
  - ❌ On coverpoint: returns exact numerator / denominator
  - ❌ On cross: returns exact numerator / denominator
  - ❌ On covergroup: aggregated bins from all coverpoints and crosses

> **Section summary:** `get_coverage()` compiles and links but always returns 0.0 — the
> instance registry required for type-level aggregation is not yet implemented (`TODO` in source).

### 6.3 `get_inst_coverage()`

- ✅ Called on covergroup instance
- ✅ Called on coverpoint of a covergroup instance
- ✅ Called on cross of a covergroup instance
- ❌ With optional `(ref int covered, ref int total)` arguments — two-argument overload not implemented
- ✅ Returns 0.0 if no coverage bins (weight != 0)
- ❌ Returns 100.0 if weight == 0 — zero-weight shortcut not implemented

> **Section summary:** Single-argument `get_inst_coverage()` is fully supported on all
> coverage objects. Two-argument form and weight==0 shortcut are unimplemented.

### 6.4 `set_inst_name(string)`

- ⚠️ Sets the name of the covergroup instance — stub method; empty body; no effect on database

> **Section summary:** Stub only; no functional effect.

---

## 7. System Tasks and Functions

- ❌ `$set_coverage_db_name(filename)` — `ERROR_RSVD_WORD` in lexer; compile error
- ❌ `$load_coverage_db(filename)` — same
- ❌ `$get_coverage()` — same; returns overall coverage across all covergroup types
  - ❌ Returns 100.0 if no covergroup instances exist
  - ❌ Returns 100.0 if all covergroups have weight = 0

> **Section summary:** All three coverage system tasks/functions are unsupported — the lexer
> treats them as reserved words and emits a compile error.

---

## 8. Coverage Computation

### 8.1 Coverpoint Coverage

- ✅ User-defined bins: `|covered| / |total|`
- ✅ Automatic bins: `|covered| / min(auto_bin_max, 2^M)`
- ✅ Empty bins excluded from both numerator and denominator
- ✅ `at_least` threshold: bin not "covered" unless hit count >= `at_least`
- ❌ Multi-instance `at_least`: uses maximum across all instances — no instance aggregation
- ⚠️ Coverpoint with zero-denominator: weight != 0 → returns 0.0; weight == 0 → 100.0 — returns 0.0 correctly; weight==0 shortcut missing

> **Section summary:** Per-instance coverpoint coverage is correct. Multi-instance aggregation
> and the weight==0 shortcut are unimplemented.

### 8.2 Cross Coverage

- ✅ Cross bins covered / total
- ✅ `ignore_bins` and `illegal_bins` excluded from denominator
- ⚠️ Cross with zero-denominator handling — returns 0.0; weight==0 shortcut missing

> **Section summary:** Per-instance cross coverage computation is correct. Weight==0 shortcut
> is unimplemented.

### 8.3 Covergroup Coverage (weighted average)

- ❌ Weighted average of coverpoint and cross coverages — equal weight given to all items
- ❌ Items with `weight = 0` excluded from numerator and denominator
- ⚠️ Zero-denominator handling — returns 0.0 for empty covergroup; weight==0 not handled

> **Section summary:** Weighted average is not implemented; all coverpoints and crosses are
> treated with equal weight. The `option.weight` field has no effect.

### 8.4 Type Coverage (`merge_instances = false`)

- ❌ Type coverage = weighted average of instance coverages (using `option.weight`) — no instance registry; `get_coverage()` always returns 0.0

> **Section summary:** Unsupported — no instance registry infrastructure. See `TODO` at
> line 96 of `V3CoverageFunctional.cpp`.

### 8.5 Type Coverage (`merge_instances = true`)

- ❌ Type coverage = union of bins from all instances
- ❌ Bin overlap determined by bin name
  - ❌ `binname` (scalar): overlaps in all instances
  - ❌ `binname[value]` (open array): overlap by value
  - ❌ `binname[N]` (fixed array): overlap by index
  - ❌ `auto[value]` / `auto[lo:hi]`: overlap by value/range
  - ❌ `binname[transition]` (transition open array): overlap by transition
  - ❌ Cross auto bin `<bn1,...,bnN>`: overlap by cross product name
- ❌ Cumulative count of overlapping bin = sum of counts across instances

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported — same root cause as §8.4.

---

## 9. Feature Combinations

### 9.1 `iff` Combinations

- ✅ `iff` on `coverpoint`
- ❌ `iff` on `cross` — `COVERIGN` "cross iff condition"; guard dropped
- ❌ `iff` on a state `bins` item (per-bin guard) — parsed, not applied in codegen
- ❌ `iff` on a transition `bins` item (per-bin guard) — parsed, not applied
- ❌ `iff` on `ignore_bins` (coverpoint) — same
- ❌ `iff` on `illegal_bins` (coverpoint) — same
- ❌ `iff` on a cross `bins_selection` (per-bin guard) — cross select expressions unsupported
- ⚠️ `iff` referencing a covergroup formal argument — structural support; untested combination
- ❌ `iff` on `coverpoint` + `iff` on individual `bins` (nested guards) — per-bin `iff` not applied
- ❌ `iff` on `cross` + `iff` on cross `bins` (nested guards) — both unsupported

> **Section summary:** `iff` is fully supported only at the coverpoint level. Per-bin `iff`
> (on state bins, transition bins, and cross bins) and `iff` on cross are unimplemented.

### 9.2 `wildcard` Combinations

- ✅ `wildcard` on value state bins
- ❌ `wildcard` on transition bins — `COVERIGN` "cover bin 'wildcard' trans list"
- ✅ `wildcard` + `ignore_bins`
- ✅ `wildcard` + `illegal_bins`
- ✅ `wildcard` + open array (`[]`)

> **Section summary:** Wildcard value bins are well-supported. Wildcard transition bins
> are unsupported.

### 9.3 `ignore_bins` Combinations

- ✅ `ignore_bins` (values) + automatic bins (values removed from auto bins)
- ✅ `ignore_bins` (values) + explicit state bins (values removed from those bins)
- ✅ `ignore_bins` (transition) + transition bins (overlapping sequences removed)
- ✅ `ignore_bins` + `illegal_bins` on same coverpoint
- ❌ `ignore_bins` on cross + `binsof` select expression — `binsof` unsupported
- ❌ `ignore_bins` on cross overriding a user `bins` — cross select expressions unsupported

> **Section summary:** `ignore_bins` within a coverpoint is fully supported. Cross-level
> `ignore_bins` is entirely unsupported.

### 9.4 `illegal_bins` Combinations

- ✅ `illegal_bins` (values) + automatic bins
- ✅ `illegal_bins` (values) + explicit state bins
- ✅ `illegal_bins` (transition) + transition bins
- ❌ `illegal_bins` on cross + `binsof` select expression — `binsof` unsupported
- ❌ `illegal_bins` takes precedence over `ignore_bins` (cross) — cross select expressions unsupported

> **Section summary:** `illegal_bins` within a coverpoint is fully supported. Cross-level
> `illegal_bins` is entirely unsupported.

### 9.5 Covergroup Formal Arguments Combinations

- ✅ Formal argument in coverpoint expression
- ✅ Formal argument in bin range
- ⚠️ Formal argument in `iff` guard expression — structural support; untested
- 🔲 Formal argument in `option.*` assignment — parsed; option not applied in codegen
- ❌ `ref` formal argument (value tracks changes after construction)
- ❌ Formal argument in clocking event — class-member clocking unsupported

> **Section summary:** `input` formal arguments in coverpoint expressions and bin ranges are
> supported. `ref` args and their use in clocking events are unimplemented.

### 9.6 `with` Filter Expression Combinations

- ❌ `with` on coverpoint state bins + range list — `COVERIGN`
- ❌ `with` on coverpoint state bins + coverpoint name (all values) — `COVERIGN`
- ❌ `with` on cross bins (`select_expr with (cond)`) — `COVERIGN`
- ❌ `with` + `matches N` on cross bins
- ❌ `with` + `matches $` on cross bins
- ❌ `with` + `binsof` on cross bins
- ❌ `type_option.distribute_first` + `with` on coverpoint bins

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported — all `with` filter expressions emit `COVERIGN`.

### 9.7 Transition Bin Combinations

- ❌ Transition bins + `iff` per-bin guard — per-bin `iff` not applied
- ❌ Transition bins + `wildcard` — `COVERIGN`
- ✅ Transition bins + `ignore_bins` (transition)
- ✅ Transition bins + `illegal_bins` (transition)
- ❌ Consecutive repetition (`[*]`) + sequence continuation — `E_UNSUPPORTED`
- ❌ Goto repetition (`[->]`) + sequence continuation — `E_UNSUPPORTED`
- ❌ Nonconsecutive repetition (`[=]`) + sequence continuation — `E_UNSUPPORTED`
- ❌ Transition bins in cross (`binsof` uses last value of transition) — `binsof` unsupported
- ❌ `default sequence` bin + other transition bins — `COVERIGN`

> **Section summary:** Basic and multi-step transition bins with `ignore`/`illegal` are
> supported. Repetition operators, wildcard transitions, per-bin `iff`, `default sequence`,
> and cross interaction are all unsupported.

### 9.8 Real Coverpoint Combinations

- ❌ Real coverpoint + value bins
- ❌ Real coverpoint + range bins (interval division)
- ❌ Real coverpoint + `+/-` tolerance
- ❌ Real coverpoint + `+%-` relative tolerance
- ❌ Real coverpoint + open array bins (`[]`)
- ❌ Real coverpoint + fixed array bins (`[N]`)
- ❌ Real coverpoint + `$` in range (single bin)
- ❌ Real coverpoint + `default` bin
- ❌ Real coverpoint + `type_option.real_interval` override
- ❌ Real coverpoint in cross
- ❌ Real coverpoint + `iff` guard
- ❌ Real coverpoint: no automatic bins (at least one explicit bin required)
- ❌ Real coverpoint: `wildcard` not allowed (constraint)
- ❌ Real coverpoint: transition bins not allowed (constraint)
- ❌ Real coverpoint: `with` filter not allowed on bins (constraint)

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported — no real-type expression handling exists
> anywhere in the coverage implementation.

### 9.9 Cross + `binsof` Combinations

- ❌ `binsof(coverpoint)` — entire coverpoint
- ❌ `binsof(coverpoint.bin)` — specific bin
- ❌ `binsof(coverpoint) intersect { range }` — filtered by value range
- ❌ `!binsof(...)` — complement
- ❌ `binsof(...) && binsof(...)`
- ❌ `binsof(...) || binsof(...)`
- ❌ `binsof` + `with` expression
- ❌ `binsof` + `with` + `matches`
- ❌ `ignore_bins` using `binsof`
- ❌ `illegal_bins` using `binsof`

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported — all `binsof` expressions produce `COVERIGN`
> "coverage select expression 'binsof'".

### 9.10 `sample()` Override Combinations

- ✅ Overridden `sample` + coverpoint expression using sample argument
- ⚠️ Overridden `sample` + `iff` guard using sample argument — `iff` on coverpoint works; use of sample arg in `iff` untested
- ❌ Overridden `sample` called from property / assertion
- ❌ Overridden `sample` called from automatic function
- ✅ Overridden `sample` + `cross` (arguments used in crossed coverpoints)

> **Section summary:** Overridden `sample(args)` with coverpoints and cross is supported.
> Invocation from assertions or automatic functions is unimplemented.

### 9.11 `per_instance` / `get_inst_coverage` Combinations

- 🔲 `per_instance = 1` + `get_inst_coverage()` on covergroup — `per_instance` has no effect; method always works
- ❌ `merge_instances = 1` + `get_inst_coverage` option — no instance aggregation
- ❌ `per_instance = 0` — `get_inst_coverage()` equals `get_coverage()` — `get_coverage()` always returns 0.0

> **Section summary:** `per_instance` is parsed only; `get_inst_coverage()` always works
> regardless of this flag. `merge_instances` and type-level aggregation are unimplemented.

### 9.12 `merge_instances` Combinations

- ❌ `merge_instances = 1` + bins with same name across instances (overlap merging)
- ❌ `merge_instances = 1` + `ignore_bins` (auto bin names unaffected)
- ❌ `merge_instances = 1` + fixed-count bins `[N]` (overlap by index)
- ❌ `merge_instances = 1` + open-array bins `[]` (overlap by value)
- ❌ `merge_instances = 1` + transition bins (overlap by transition sequence)
- ❌ `merge_instances = 1` + cross auto bins (overlap by cross product name)

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported — no instance registry or bin-merging
> infrastructure exists.

### 9.13 Embedded Covergroup + Inheritance Combinations

- ❌ Embedded covergroup + `extends` overrides coverpoint bins
- ❌ Embedded covergroup + `extends` overrides `option.*`
- ❌ Embedded covergroup + `extends` adds new coverpoint then crosses with inherited one
- ❌ Embedded covergroup + `local` class member in overridden coverpoint

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported — `extends` is silently dropped.

### 9.14 Clocking Block Signal in Coverpoint

- ❌ Coverpoint of clocking block signal with `#1step` skew (Preponed region)
- ❌ Coverpoint of clocking block signal with `#0` skew (Observed region)

> **Section summary:** Unsupported — no scheduling-region handling for clocking-block
> signal references in coverpoints.

### 9.15 Block Event Expression Combinations

- ❌ `@@(begin task_name)` + coverpoints referencing task-local expressions
- ❌ `@@(end func_name)` + multiple coverpoints
- ❌ `@@(begin ...)` or `@@(end ...)` + `strobe` option (strobe has no effect)

> **Section summary:** Entirely unsupported — `COVERIGN` "coverage '@@' events".

### 9.16 Covergroup in a Package

- ❌ Covergroup declared in a package and instantiated in a module
- ❌ `package::CgType::type_option` access

> **Section summary:** Unsupported — no tests exist and package-scope covergroups have
> not been validated.

### 9.17 Coverage Options Inheritance (covergroup → coverpoint/cross defaults)

- ✅ `option.auto_bin_max` set at covergroup, applies to all coverpoints
- ✅ `option.auto_bin_max` at covergroup, overridden at specific coverpoint
- ✅ `option.at_least` at covergroup, applies to all coverpoints and crosses
- 🔲 `option.detect_overlap` set at covergroup, applies to all coverpoints
- 🔲 `option.cross_num_print_missing` at covergroup, overridden at specific cross
- 🔲 `option.cross_retain_auto_bins` at covergroup, overridden at specific cross
- ⚠️ `option.weight`, `option.goal`, `option.comment`, `option.per_instance` do **not** cascade to lower levels — they don't cascade (correct), but they also have no effect at their own level

> **Section summary:** `auto_bin_max` and `at_least` inheritance from covergroup to
> coverpoints/crosses is supported. All other option inheritance is parsed only.

---

*Reference: IEEE Std 1800-2023, Clause 19 — Functional Coverage*
*Assessment based on source as of 2026-02-19*
